title: Science Basics O/X
description: Everyday facts about the natural world

cards.prompt: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
cards.explanation: At standard pressure, water turns to ice at 0 degrees Celsius (32 degrees Fahrenheit).

cards.prompt: The Sun is a star.
cards.explanation: The Sun is the star at the center of our Solar System, producing light and heat by nuclear fusion.

cards.prompt: Humans breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide.
cards.explanation: In respiration, we take in oxygen and release carbon dioxide as a waste product.

cards.prompt: Plants make their own food through photosynthesis.
cards.explanation: Plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to make food through photosynthesis.

cards.prompt: The chemical formula for water is H2O.
cards.explanation: Each water molecule is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, written as H2O.

cards.prompt: Sound travels slower than light.
cards.explanation: Light travels far faster (about 300,000 km/s) than sound (about 340 m/s in air).

cards.prompt: The Earth is the largest planet in the Solar System.
cards.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet; the Earth is much smaller.

cards.prompt: A healthy human has two hearts.
cards.explanation: A human normally has one heart that pumps blood around the body.

cards.prompt: The Sun revolves around the Earth.
cards.explanation: The Earth and other planets orbit the Sun, not the other way around.

cards.prompt: Bats are a kind of bird.
cards.explanation: Bats can fly, but they are mammals that feed milk to their young, not birds.

cards.prompt: Diamond is made of gold.
cards.explanation: Diamond is made of carbon atoms, not gold.

cards.prompt: A year on Earth is exactly 100 days long.
cards.explanation: A year on Earth is about 365 days, the time it takes to orbit the Sun once.

cards.prompt: A bolt of lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun.
cards.explanation: Lightning can heat the air to around 30,000 degrees Celsius, several times hotter than the Sun's surface, which is about 5,500 degrees Celsius.

cards.prompt: Humans use only 10 percent of their brains.
cards.explanation: This is a popular myth. Brain scans show that virtually all parts of the brain are active, and we use the whole brain over the course of a day.

cards.prompt: Goldfish can only remember things for three seconds.
cards.explanation: Goldfish actually have memories lasting weeks or months; they can even be trained to respond to signals.

cards.prompt: More than half of the adult human body is water.
cards.explanation: Water makes up roughly 60 percent of an adult's body weight, filling cells, blood, and other fluids.

cards.prompt: Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
cards.explanation: Sound needs a medium like air, water, or solids to travel. In the empty vacuum of space there is nothing to carry the vibrations.

cards.prompt: Penguins live in the wild at the North Pole.
cards.explanation: Wild penguins live only in the Southern Hemisphere, such as Antarctica; polar bears, not penguins, live in the Arctic.

cards.prompt: An octopus has three hearts.
cards.explanation: An octopus has one main heart that pumps blood around the body and two extra hearts that pump blood through the gills.

cards.prompt: Earth's seasons are caused by the planet being closer to the Sun in summer.
cards.explanation: Seasons are caused by the tilt of Earth's axis, which changes how directly sunlight hits each hemisphere, not by the distance to the Sun.

cards.prompt: The blood inside your veins is blue.
cards.explanation: Blood is always red; it is darker when low in oxygen. Veins only look bluish because of how light passes through skin.

cards.prompt: Venus is the hottest planet in the Solar System.
cards.explanation: Although Mercury is closer to the Sun, Venus is hotter because its thick carbon dioxide atmosphere traps heat, keeping the surface around 460 degrees Celsius.

cards.prompt: The Moon's gravity is the main cause of ocean tides on Earth.
cards.explanation: The Moon's gravitational pull on Earth's oceans is the main driver of the tides, with the Sun adding a smaller effect.

cards.prompt: Botanically, a tomato is a vegetable.
cards.explanation: Botanically, a tomato is a fruit because it develops from a flower and contains seeds, even though it is used as a vegetable in cooking.

cards.prompt: An adult human skeleton normally has 206 bones.
cards.explanation: Babies are born with about 300 bones, but many fuse during growth, leaving an adult with 206 bones.

cards.prompt: Sharks are fish.
cards.explanation: Sharks are fish with skeletons made of cartilage instead of bone, and they breathe through gills like other fish.

cards.prompt: Antibiotics can cure illnesses caused by viruses.
cards.explanation: Antibiotics only work against bacteria. Viral illnesses like colds and flu are not cured by antibiotics.

cards.prompt: Sound travels faster in air than in water.
cards.explanation: Sound travels about four times faster in water than in air, because water molecules are packed closer together.

cards.prompt: Spiders have eight legs.
cards.explanation: Spiders are arachnids with eight legs, which sets them apart from insects, which have six.

cards.prompt: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
cards.explanation: Lightning often strikes the same spot repeatedly; tall structures like skyscrapers are hit many times every year.

cards.prompt: Iron is attracted to magnets.
cards.explanation: Iron is a ferromagnetic metal, which means magnets pull on it strongly; many other metals, like copper, are not attracted.

cards.prompt: Bulls charge because the color red makes them angry.
cards.explanation: Cattle cannot see red the way humans do. Bulls react to the movement of the cape, not its color.

cards.prompt: Ostriches bury their heads in the sand when they are scared.
cards.explanation: This is a myth. Ostriches may lower their heads to the ground or run away, but they do not bury their heads in sand.

cards.prompt: Earth's atmosphere is made up mostly of nitrogen.
cards.explanation: Air is about 78 percent nitrogen and about 21 percent oxygen, with small amounts of other gases.

cards.prompt: A rainbow forms when sunlight is bent and reflected by water droplets.
cards.explanation: Raindrops refract and reflect sunlight, splitting it into its different colors and creating a rainbow.

cards.prompt: Early humans lived at the same time as dinosaurs like Tyrannosaurus rex.
cards.explanation: Non-bird dinosaurs died out about 66 million years ago, tens of millions of years before the first humans appeared.
